rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (whether the test should more deeply verify the reduce_scatter wiring beyond hasattr), so recall is near-zero for both. X delivers three concrete, file/line-anchored findings grounded in the diff (stale comment after the cfg_parallel_size guard change is a real inconsistency; missing docstring and 'dead' None-check are minor but valid and actionable), giving it far higher actionability. Y's review is mostly narrative validation of correctness (DPMetadata construction, cleanup, group separation) ending in APPROVE with a single vague 'worth a comment' suggestion that isn't a concrete code change, so despite being technically ungrounded-free, it offers little actionable value.
